description:
You are a FinOps analyst who has just received two month-end AWS cost and usage CSV reports for Q3-2023.  
    Your goal is to use nothing but standard GNU/Linux shell utilities – in particular **awk** and **sed** (you may combine them with other core‐utils where it helps) – to transform, aggregate, and summarise those raw files.

    Starting point
    1. Two raw CSV files already exist:
          • /home/user/raw_reports/aws_costs_2023-08.csv  
          • /home/user/raw_reports/aws_costs_2023-09.csv  
       They both share the exact five-column header  
          Date,Account,Service,Region,CostUSD
       and every subsequent line is a single day’s spend, e.g.

          2023-08-01,123456789012,AmazonEC2,us-east-1,25.30

       Do NOT modify, move or delete these originals – they must stay intact for audit purposes.

    2. There is **no other content** in /home/user/raw_reports/ besides the two files mentioned above.

    Target deliverables (what the automated grader will check)
    A. A pipe-delimited copy of each CSV must be produced under a brand-new directory  
       /home/user/pipe_stage/  
       Naming convention: keep the original base name and change the extension to .pipe  
            • /home/user/pipe_stage/aws_costs_2023-08.pipe  
            • /home/user/pipe_stage/aws_costs_2023-09.pipe  
       Replacement rules:  
          – Replace every comma “,” with a vertical bar “|”.  
          – All other characters (including the line terminator) must remain untouched.  
       The pipe files must therefore be byte-for-byte identical to the source, except that all commas are now pipes.

    B. A **service-level summary** file located at  
       /home/user/finops_logs/q3_2023_service_costs.log  
       Format requirements:  
          1) First line is a header exactly equal to  
                Service|TotalCostUSD  
          2) Subsequent lines are the distinct values of the “Service” column aggregated across both months,  
             expressed with exactly two digits after the decimal point, **sorted in descending order of TotalCostUSD**.  
          3) Use a pipe character as the sole delimiter.  
          4) NO thousands separators, NO currency symbols; just pure numbers with two decimals.  
       Example layout (values below are illustrative, the grader will verify exact numbers):  
          Service|TotalCostUSD  
          AmazonEC2|164.60  
          AmazonRDS|28.76  
          AmazonS3|18.97  
          AmazonLambda|2.57  

    C. A **daily trend file** located at  
       /home/user/finops_logs/q3_2023_daily_trend.log  
       Format requirements:  
          1) Header line:  
                Date|TotalCostUSD  
          2) One line per distinct date (yyyy-mm-dd) present in either raw file.  
          3) Sum the CostUSD for every service on that date across the two reports.  
          4) Keep the list **chronologically ascending by date**.  
          5) Same delimiter and numeric formatting rules as in item B.  

    Environmental constraints & hints
    • You do NOT have root, stay inside /home/user.  
    • Achieve the task exclusively via command-line work; do not write scripts in other languages.  
    • You are free to stage intermediate work (e.g., temporary files) anywhere under /home/user, but only the three deliverables described above will be assessed.  
    • Because the grader parses the files literally, make sure there are no leading/trailing spaces, hidden control characters, or superfluous blank lines.  
    • Parallel or pipelined command usage is encouraged (for instance, transforming and aggregating in a single pipeline or running multiple conversions in the background), but is not mandatory – correctness is.  

    Once finished, double-check:
       1. Both .pipe files exist with exactly the same number of lines as their CSV parents.  
       2. /home/user/finops_logs/q3_2023_service_costs.log contains the header plus four data lines, sorted by cost descending.  
       3. /home/user/finops_logs/q3_2023_daily_trend.log contains the header plus six data lines, sorted by date ascending.  
    The automated tests will fail if any spelling, delimiter, ordering, or numeric formatting deviates from the specification.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing raw data for grading -->
    <preexisting_files>
        <file path="/home/user/raw_reports/aws_costs_2023-08.csv"><![CDATA[
Date,Account,Service,Region,CostUSD
2023-08-01,123456789012,AmazonEC2,us-east-1,25.30
2023-08-01,123456789012,AmazonS3,us-east-1,3.76
2023-08-02,123456789012,AmazonEC2,us-east-1,26.10
2023-08-02,123456789012,AmazonS3,us-east-1,3.59
2023-08-03,123456789012,AmazonEC2,us-east-1,24.87
2023-08-03,123456789012,AmazonRDS,us-east-1,14.05
2023-08-03,123456789012,AmazonLambda,us-east-1,1.22
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/raw_reports/aws_costs_2023-09.csv"><![CDATA[
Date,Account,Service,Region,CostUSD
2023-09-01,123456789012,AmazonEC2,us-east-1,29.44
2023-09-01,123456789012,AmazonS3,us-east-1,3.85
2023-09-01,123456789012,AmazonRDS,us-east-1,14.71
2023-09-02,123456789012,AmazonEC2,us-east-1,30.02
2023-09-02,123456789012,AmazonS3,us-east-1,3.99
2023-09-02,123456789012,AmazonLambda,us-east-1,1.35
2023-09-03,123456789012,AmazonEC2,us-east-1,28.87
2023-09-03,123456789012,AmazonS3,us-east-1,3.78
]]></file>
    </preexisting_files>

    <!-- Expected deliverables for verification -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/pipe_stage/aws_costs_2023-08.pipe">
            <!-- identical to 08.csv with commas replaced by pipes -->
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/pipe_stage/aws_costs_2023-09.pipe">
            <!-- identical to 09.csv with commas replaced by pipes -->
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/finops_logs/q3_2023_service_costs.log"><![CDATA[
Service|TotalCostUSD
AmazonEC2|164.60
AmazonRDS|28.76
AmazonS3|18.97
AmazonLambda|2.57
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/finops_logs/q3_2023_daily_trend.log"><![CDATA[
Date|TotalCostUSD
2023-08-01|29.06
2023-08-02|29.69
2023-08-03|40.14
2023-09-01|48.00
2023-09-02|35.36
2023-09-03|32.65
]]></file>
    </expected_files>
